description:
You are a junior compliance analyst.  
  A lead auditor has provisioned a small working directory tree that mimics a production server and wants you to:

  1. Harden the sample SSH configuration.
  2. Produce a pair of **parallel** audit logs that capture open-port and running-service data already staged for you.
  3. Consolidate the results into a single JSON‐formatted compliance report.
  4. Leave behind a simple, timestamped action log so the auditor can trace what you did.

  Everything happens under the fixed base directory
  /home/user/compliance

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  1) Harden the sample SSH configuration  
     File to edit: /home/user/compliance/configs/sshd_config  
     • Change the value of PermitRootLogin from “yes” to “no”.  
     • Change the value of PasswordAuthentication from “yes” to “no”.  
     • Do **not** remove, reorder or duplicate any other existing
       lines.  
     • Save the file in-place.

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  2) Create two small shell scripts and run them **concurrently**  
     Script folder: /home/user/compliance/scripts  
     
     a) port_audit.sh  
        - Reads exactly the file  
          /home/user/compliance/reference/ports_sample.log  
        - Copies its entire content, unmodified, to the output file  
          /home/user/compliance/logs/port_audit.log  

     b) service_audit.sh  
        - Reads exactly the file  
          /home/user/compliance/reference/services_sample.log  
        - Copies its entire content, unmodified, to the output file  
          /home/user/compliance/logs/service_audit.log  

     Both scripts must:  
        • create the /home/user/compliance/logs directory if it
          does not already exist;  
        • be given execute permission (chmod u+x);  
        • be launched in parallel from a single shell command
          line (background jobs, & or similar).  
        • The terminal should not return control until *both*
          scripts have finished writing their respective log
          files.

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  3) Build the consolidated compliance report  
     Location: /home/user/compliance/report/compliance_report.json  
     Required JSON structure **exactly**:

     {
       "timestamp": "<ISO-8601-UTC-STAMP>",
       "port_audit_log": "logs/port_audit.log",
       "service_audit_log": "logs/service_audit.log",
       "total_listening_ports": 3,
       "total_unique_processes": 4
     }

     Notes:  
       • timestamp must be in the form YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (UTC).  
       • total_listening_ports is the number of *lines* found in
         port_audit.log.  
       • total_unique_processes is the number of *lines* found in
         service_audit.log.  
       • Use the *relative* paths shown above (they are part of the
         compliance data model).  

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  4) Leave an action trail  
     File: /home/user/compliance/logs/action.log  

     Append exactly three lines, **in this order**, each line ending
     with a newline:

       <ISO-8601-UTC-STAMP> MODIFY_CONFIG
       <ISO-8601-UTC-STAMP> GENERATE_REPORT
       <ISO-8601-UTC-STAMP> DONE

     Only the timestamps should differ between the lines.  The test
     harness will simply look for the tokens
     “MODIFY_CONFIG”, “GENERATE_REPORT” and “DONE” in that order on
     consecutive lines.

  --------------------------------------------------------------------
  Summary of artefacts that will be checked by the automated grader:
  • /home/user/compliance/configs/sshd_config    (edited contents)  
  • /home/user/compliance/scripts/port_audit.sh  (exists, executable)  
  • /home/user/compliance/scripts/service_audit.sh (exists, executable)  
  • /home/user/compliance/logs/port_audit.log    (3 exact lines)  
  • /home/user/compliance/logs/service_audit.log (4 exact lines)  
  • /home/user/compliance/report/compliance_report.json (exact keys & values)  
  • /home/user/compliance/logs/action.log        (3 lines, correct tokens)  

  Do **not** remove, rename or relocate any pre-existing files or
  directories.  You do not need super-user privileges to complete
  this task.
truth:
Pre-existing structure before the agent starts:

  /home/user/compliance/
      configs/
          sshd_config
      reference/
          ports_sample.log
          services_sample.log

  1. /home/user/compliance/configs/sshd_config  (initial content)

     # Example SSHD Configuration
     Port 22
     PermitRootLogin yes
     PasswordAuthentication yes
     ChallengeResponseAuthentication yes

  2. /home/user/compliance/reference/ports_sample.log  (exact content)

     tcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:22            0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN
     tcp        0      0 127.0.0.1:631         0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN
     udp        0      0 0.0.0.0:68            0.0.0.0:*              

  3. /home/user/compliance/reference/services_sample.log  (exact content)

     sshd
     cupsd
     dhclient
     systemd

  Expected final state after the agent finishes:

  A) /home/user/compliance/configs/sshd_config  must now include:

     PermitRootLogin no
     PasswordAuthentication no

     and **must NOT** contain any line reading 
     “PermitRootLogin yes” or “PasswordAuthentication yes”.

  B) New directories created by the agent:

     /home/user/compliance/scripts
     /home/user/compliance/logs
     /home/user/compliance/report

  C) Newly created files:

     1. /home/user/compliance/scripts/port_audit.sh   (mode 755)
     2. /home/user/compliance/scripts/service_audit.sh (mode 755)

     The grader will only verify that both files exist and have the
     executable bit set.

     3. /home/user/compliance/logs/port_audit.log  

        Must be *byte-for-byte* identical to
        /home/user/compliance/reference/ports_sample.log  
        (3 lines total).

     4. /home/user/compliance/logs/service_audit.log  

        Must be *byte-for-byte* identical to
        /home/user/compliance/reference/services_sample.log  
        (4 lines total).

     5. /home/user/compliance/report/compliance_report.json  

        The grader will parse the file and verify:  
          - Keys: timestamp, port_audit_log, service_audit_log,
            total_listening_ports, total_unique_processes  
          - port_audit_log == "logs/port_audit.log"  
          - service_audit_log == "logs/service_audit.log"  
          - total_listening_ports == 3  
          - total_unique_processes == 4  

        The timestamp value only needs to be syntactically valid;
        it will not be compared against a fixed moment.

     6. /home/user/compliance/logs/action.log  

        Verification rules:  
          - Exactly 3 non-empty lines  
          - Line-1 ends with “MODIFY_CONFIG”  
          - Line-2 ends with “GENERATE_REPORT”  
          - Line-3 ends with “DONE”  
          - Tokens must appear in that exact order on consecutive
            lines.

  No other file content is tested.
